mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals improving household income and aggregate demand, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% while its 3-month change is positive, rising discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have mean 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional mean or if the negative hit rate is below 55% across walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals inflation-driven tightening; the joint pressure on margins and discount rates produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS change has a nonnegative coefficient or if triggered 60-day returns are not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS signals strengthening consumer spending, improving near-term revenue expectations for listed firms and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not generate higher average 20-day returns than negative RSAFS momentum in at least 60% of walk-forward splits.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and interest-sensitive demand, leading investors to mark down cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break signals are followed by nonnegative average 60-day returns or if negative-return hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative turn in INDPRO over 3 months signals declining factory output and weaker operating leverage, causing earnings revisions to fall and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO 3-month changes are not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than positive INDPRO changes, or if Rank IC is nonnegative in most rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE below 4% combined with rising FEDFUNDS identifies a late-cycle tightening regime in which wage pressure and discount-rate increases compress equity multiples, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0 and FEDFUNDS rises at least 25bp over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-FEDFUNDS condition has no incremental negative predictive power versus FEDFUNDS alone or if triggered 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth paired with stable CPIAUCSL signals real demand expansion without an inflation penalty, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is below its 12-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the positive GDPC1 and benign CPIAUCSL interaction is followed by lower 5-day returns than unconditional returns, or if its coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.
